description:
Create a classification schedule for Diethyl Ether, Toluene, o-Xylene, Acetic Acid, Benzaldehyde, and Glycerine by retrieving their Flash Point and Boiling Point from CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/). Determine the NFPA 30 Flammable Liquid Class for each chemical using the following criteria in Fahrenheit:

- Class IA: Flash Point < 73°F AND Boiling Point < 100°F
- Class IB: Flash Point < 73°F AND Boiling Point >= 100°F
- Class IC: Flash Point >= 73°F AND Flash Point < 100°F
- Class II: Flash Point >= 100°F AND Flash Point < 140°F
- Class IIIA: Flash Point >= 140°F AND Flash Point < 200°F
- Class IIIB: Flash Point >= 200°F

Export the data to a CSV file at `~/Documents/flammable_liquid_classes.csv` with the headers: `Chemical Name`, `Flash Point (F)`, `Boiling Point (F)`, and `NFPA 30 Class`. Record numeric values for temperatures and the full class string (e.g., Class IB).
